mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals increasing labor market tightness that historically precedes monetary policy tightening and compression of equity valuations; therefore, decreases in UNRATE reliably predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly change in UNRATE and forward 20-day return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window, or the sign flips in more than 40% of overlapping periods.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase of 25 basis points or more in the federal funds rate from its cyclical trough signals the onset of a monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS cumulative increase >= 25bp from cycle low
failure_condition: After trigger activation, the mean 60-day forward return is not negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05) or rank IC of the tightening signal is less than 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) decline concurrently with the federal funds rate remaining at or below 0.25%, it signals that accommodative monetary policy is failing to stimulate housing demand, indicating underlying economic weakness that forecasts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold (HOUST declines MoM and FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%), the mean forward 20-day return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) or the interaction term in a regression model (with main effects) is not statistically significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Within a low inflation environment (year-over-year CPIAUCSL below 2%), an increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding economic output without immediate inflation risk, which boosts corporate profit expectations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with CPIAUCSL YoY < 2%, the rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change on forward 20-day returns is less than 0.05, or the signal is not consistently positive across multiple low-inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) directly measures consumer spending momentum, which is a primary driver of corporate earnings and thus predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly change in RSAFS and forward 20-day return is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over the entire evaluation period, or rolling 2-year rank IC falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When non-farm payroll employment (PAYEMS) monthly change exceeds 200,000, it signals robust labor demand and income growth that boosts consumer spending and equity markets, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: After a trigger event, the mean forward 5-day return is not significantly positive (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05), or the win rate is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising industrial production (INDPRO) and declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates structural imbalances where manufacturing growth is not supported by housing demand, creating economic uncertainty that depresses equity markets over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO and HOUST (with opposite signed changes) is not a statistically significant predictor of 60-day returns (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression, or the signal does not produce negative mean returns in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: During the zero lower bound period when the federal funds rate is at or below 0.25%, an increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) reduces real interest rates and stimulates aggregate demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%, the monthly change in CPIAUCSL does not have a positive rank IC above 0.04 on 20-day forward returns, or the directional accuracy is below 55%.
